name: Beelink Mini S12 Mini PC (Intel N95)
shortDescription: The Beelink Mini S12 is the most affordable entry point for running local AI in 2026. Powered by Intel's 12th Gen N95 processor with 8GB DDR4 RAM, it handles 7B models via CPU-only inference in Ollama — slowly but reliably. At under $160, it's the cheapest way to run a private, offline AI assistant 24/7.
useCases: Always-on local AI assistant (7B models via Ollama)
Private offline chatbot for home or office
Windows 11 AI app experimentation on a budget
Low-power home server for light AI tasks
pros: Under $160 — the cheapest path to running a local LLM
15W TDP — runs 24/7 without meaningful electricity cost
Windows 11 Home included — plug-and-play with Ollama and LM Studio
Dual HDMI 4K output — doubles as a capable desktop PC
Compact size — fits anywhere, silent under light load
cons: 8GB RAM caps you at 7B Q4 models — no headroom for 13B or larger
CPU-only inference — no GPU acceleration, expect 2–5 tokens/second
N95 iGPU is too weak for Stable Diffusion at usable speeds
Not upgradeable — soldered RAM limits future expansion
5–10x slower than the GMKtec NucBox M5 Pro for AI tasks
verdict: The Beelink Mini S12 is for one specific buyer: someone who wants to run a private 7B AI assistant around the clock and has a tight budget. It will not impress you with speed — 2–5 tokens/second on CPU is slow — but it works, it's always on, and it costs less than a dinner out. If speed matters at all, spend more on the GMKtec NucBox M5 Pro. If the budget is genuinely $160 and you just want to get started, this is your entry ticket.

faq.question: Can the Beelink Mini S12 run local LLMs?
faq.answer: Yes, but slowly. Using Ollama on Windows 11, the Mini S12 runs Llama 3.1 8B at approximately 2–5 tokens/second using the CPU. The 8GB RAM limits you to 7B Q4 models. It works for a private offline chatbot but is not suitable for interactive use cases that require fast responses.

faq.question: How does the Beelink Mini S12 compare to the GMKtec NucBox M5 Pro?
faq.answer: The GMKtec NucBox M5 Pro is significantly faster — its AMD Ryzen 9 6900HX with 32GB RAM achieves 8–15 tokens/second CPU-side, and the Radeon 780M iGPU can push 20–35 tokens/second with ROCm on Linux. The Mini S12 is slower (2–5 t/s), has less RAM (8GB vs 32GB), and cannot run 13B models. The GMKtec costs more but delivers a far better AI experience.

faq.question: Can I upgrade the RAM in the Beelink Mini S12?
faq.answer: No. The Intel N95's memory is soldered to the board in the Mini S12 configuration — it cannot be upgraded. If you need more than 8GB, consider the GMKtec NucBox M5 Pro which uses standard SO-DIMM slots and ships with 32GB.
